title: 1990 Tribunal Fundraiser & Registration Letter
authors: Daniel Berrigan; Margaret Randall; Rafael Cancel Miranda
description: September 15, 1990 registration packet and fundraising appeal for the Special International Tribunal on Human Rights Violations of Political Prisoners and POWs in U.S. Prisons, held December 7-10, 1990 at Hunter College, New York City. Signatories include Daniel Berrigan, Margaret Randall, and Rafael Cancel Miranda. Companion piece to the Dec 7 1990 Chicano Political Prisoners submission already in the archive.

title: 6th Anniversary of the Assassination of George Jackson — San Quentin Demonstration
description: August 21 Coalition demonstration flyer commemorating the 6th anniversary of George Jackson's August 21, 1971 killing by San Quentin guards. Includes a fact sheet on white-supremacist attacks against Black prisoners in the California system.

title: About the New York Three
authors: NY3 Freedom Campaign West Coast Committee
publisher: NY3 Freedom Campaign West Coast Committee
description: Informational pamphlet from the New York Three Freedom Campaign West Coast Committee on the case of Black Panther political prisoners Herman Bell, Anthony 'Jalil' Bottom, and Albert 'Nuh' Washington — convicted of the 1971 deaths of NYPD officers Waverly Jones and Joseph Piagentini.

title: A Child Lost in the Storm — Eduardo Galeano (trans. Marilyn Buck)
description: Personal essay by Uruguayan writer Eduardo Galeano, translated from Spanish by anti-imperialist political prisoner Marilyn Buck. Originally published in El País on March 28, 1990. One of the literary works produced from inside Buck's federal incarceration.

title: Ain't Nothing Changed!!! — Ruchell Magee (1975)
description: 1975 pamphlet by Ruchell Cinque Magee — sole surviving defendant of the August 7, 1970 Marin County courthouse incident — examining the U.S. court system and the conspiracy surrounding his case. Distributed by the Eugene Coalition and the Liberation Support Movement.

title: Alabama's Atticas — Inmates for Action Statement (Atmore-Holman)
authors: Inmates for Action; Atmore-Holman Brothers Defense Committee
description: Inmates for Action / Atmore-Holman Brothers Defense Committee statement critiquing the Alabama prison system and the state repression that followed the 1974 Atmore-Holman prison rebellions and the killing of guards at the Atmore (G.K. Fountain) facility. A companion document to the Pontiac Brothers and Attica Brothers organizing of the same period.

title: A Living Tomb — The Nation on the Lexington Women's Control Unit (1988)
authors: National Campaign to Abolish the Lexington Women's Control Unit
description: March 26, 1988 reprint of The Nation's investigative report on the Federal Bureau of Prisons' Lexington High Security Unit, the basement control unit at FCI Lexington, Kentucky used to hold women political prisoners Susan Rosenberg, Alejandrina Torres, and Silvia Baraldini under sensory-deprivation conditions. Distributed by the National Campaign to Abolish the Lexington Women's Control Unit.

title: Resistance Conspiracy Case — Open Letter to the Progressive Community
description: Emergency Committee for Political Prisoners open letter on behalf of the Resistance Conspiracy Case defendants — Marilyn Buck, Alan Berkman, Tim Blunk, Susan Rosenberg, Linda Evans, and Laura Whitehorn — seeking solidarity from the broader left for the federal prosecution arising from the Armed Resistance Unit / Red Guerrilla Resistance bombings of the 1980s.

title: Resistance Conspiracy Case — Arraignment Statement of Dr. Alan Berkman
description: Resistance Conspiracy Case defendant Dr. Alan Berkman's arraignment statement framing his armed actions as resistance to U.S. government atrocities in Central America, Southern Africa, and at home. Berkman, suffering from Hodgkin's Disease, would nearly die several times in federal custody due to deliberately withheld medical treatment.

title: The Soledad Brothers — Berkeley Tribe Centerfold (July 1970)
description: July 1970 Berkeley Tribe pullout / centerfold on the Soledad Brothers case — George Jackson, Fleeta Drumgo, and John Clutchette, charged with the January 1970 retaliatory killing of Soledad State Prison guard John Mills after Mills' colleagues had shot and killed three Black prisoners (Nolen, Edwards, Miller) in the prison yard a few days earlier.

title: Prisoners in Revolution — Response to 'Prairie Fire' (1975)
authors: Jaan Laaman; John Yancey; Richard Williams; Roger Boberg
description: 1975 prisoner-authored response to the Weather Underground's 'Prairie Fire' statement, theorizing the role of prisons in U.S. imperialism. Written by future Ohio 7 / United Freedom Front members Jaan Laaman and Richard Williams together with John Yancey and Roger Boberg — among the earliest joint statements between Black and white anti-imperialist prisoners articulating a shared revolutionary politics.

title: Pontiac Prisoners Support Coalition Newsletter No. 1 (October 1978)
description: Founding newsletter of the Pontiac Prisoners Support Coalition (October 1978) — formed in the wake of the July 22, 1978 Pontiac Correctional Center rebellion in Illinois, which left three guards dead and led to the indictment of 31 'Pontiac Brothers' on capital charges. Includes the coalition's founding principles and an open letter from the Pontiac-Stateville Coordinating Committee.

title: Pontiac Prisoners Support Coalition Newsletter No. 8 (October 1979)
description: October 1979 Pontiac Brothers support newsletter covering trial venue changes, the mobilization of white-supremacist groups outside the courthouse, and the coalition's analysis of the alleged frame-up of the 31 prisoners charged with murder, conspiracy, and mob action over the 1978 Pontiac prison rebellion.

title: Black Nation's Freedom Fighters JAILED
description: Flyer about the New York 8 case with photos and information on non-collaboration with the grand jury, calling for support of Black liberation defendants facing fascist terrorism charges.

title: Brinks Trial Testimony — Sundiata Acoli (1983)
description: Trial transcript of Sundiata Acoli's testimony in the 1983 Brinks armored-truck expropriation case, situating the Revolutionary Armed Task Force action within the history of the Black Liberation Movement.

title: Buffalo Chip (Fall 1999) — Omaha Two
description: Nebraskans for Justice newsletter covering police brutality, Nebraska prison regulations, and the cases of Omaha Two political prisoners Ed Poindexter and Mondo we Langa (David Rice).

title: Buffalo Chip (Spring/Fall 2002) — Omaha Two
description: Nebraskans for Justice newsletter covering the background and chronology of the Rice/Poindexter Omaha Two case along with discussion of Nebraska pardons and paroles.

title: Black Flag — Organ of the Anarchist Black Cross (December 1976)
publisher: Black Flag Group / Anarchist Black Cross
description: December 1976 issue of the Anarchist Black Cross's Black Flag magazine focused on false-imprisonment cases in Ireland and Scotland and on broader international prisoner-support work.

title: By Any Means Necessary — Ruchell Magee Defense Committee Periodical
description: Ruchell Magee Defense Committee periodical compiling interviews, prisoner writings, and case information on Magee, the San Quentin Six, and the Soledad Brothers.

title: California Prisons — Prisoners Take Prisons to Court (1974)
description: August 5, 1974 Pacific News Service report on a prisoners' legal suit against the California prison system over conditions in San Quentin's Adjustment Center, filed in the wake of the killing of George Jackson.

title: Communication on Human Rights On Behalf of Prisoners Held in the United States (1972)
authors: Georgia Jackson; National Conference of Black Lawyers
description: 1972 petition to the United Nations from Georgia Jackson (mother of George and Jonathan Jackson) and the National Conference of Black Lawyers documenting human-rights violations against incarcerated people in the wake of George Jackson's killing.

title: Comrade George — An Investigation into the Official Story of His Assassination (1972)
description: Red Prison Movement investigation challenging the official San Quentin narrative of George Jackson's August 21, 1971 killing, with detailed timeline analysis and a centerfold poster.

title: CCPO and BAPC — Call to the Convention (December 1972)
description: Pamphlet announcing the December 2-3, 1972 Bay Area Prison Convention organized by the Coordinating Council of Prisoner Organizations, with registration details and agenda.

title: Construction Moratorium — Coordinating Council of Prisoner Organizations
description: Coordinating Council of Prisoner Organizations statement calling for a moratorium on new prison construction in California, supported by cost statistics.

title: Committee for Prisoner Support in Birmingham (Atmore-Holman)
publisher: Committee for Prisoner Support in Birmingham
description: Information pamphlet outlining the purpose and activities of the Committee for Prisoner Support in Birmingham, organizing for the Atmore-Holman Brothers in Alabama.

title: Statement of Facts — Norma Jean Croy
description: Defense-committee narrative of events surrounding the 1978 arrest of Karuk siblings Norma Jean and Patrick 'Hooty' Croy, framing the case as a wrongful prosecution rooted in anti-Native racism.

title: In Total Resistance — Statements & Poetry from Peltier, Standing Deer, and Garcia (1980)
authors: Leonard Peltier; Standing Deer; Bobby Garcia
description: Memorial booklet in honor of Bobby Gene Garcia, who died under suspicious circumstances at USP Terre Haute in December 1980, collecting statements and poetry from Garcia, Standing Deer, and Leonard Peltier.

title: Leonard Peltier Freedom Calendar 1995
description: 1995 calendar produced by the Leonard Peltier Support Group featuring artwork by Peltier and other incarcerated artists from the Crazy Horse Spirit Prisoner Arts Program along with a timeline of AIM events.

title: Eddie Hatcher Execution Date???
description: Defense-committee pamphlet on the case and imprisonment of Tuscarora activist Eddie Hatcher, raising the alarm about the possibility of his death in custody.

title: 25 Years of Struggle, 25 Years of Resistance — Document No. 1 (1978)
publisher: National Committee to Free the Four Puerto Rican Prisoners of War
description: Document submitted on behalf of the four imprisoned Puerto Rican Nationalists (Lolita Lebron, Rafael Cancel Miranda, Irvin Flores, Andres Figueroa Cordero) laying out the factual and legal basis for their release after 25 years.

title: 25 Years of Struggle, 25 Years of Resistance — Document No. 2 (Lolita Lebron, 1978)
publisher: National Committee to Free the Four Puerto Rican Prisoners of War
description: Memorandum from imprisoned Puerto Rican Nationalist Lolita Lebron on Puerto Rico and the Nationalist prisoners taken after the November 1, 1954 attack on the U.S. Capitol.

title: Civic-Religious Committee In Support of the Puerto Rican POWs
publisher: Civic-Religious Committee in Support of the Puerto Rican POWs
description: Pamphlet on the colonial status of Puerto Rico, the basis for the FALN 11 prisoners' POW claims, and grand-jury repression in the independentista movement.

title: Did You Know That… (Puerto Rican POWs Educational Packet)
publisher: National Committee to Free Puerto Rican Prisoners of War and Political Prisoners
description: Educational packet with portraits of 12 Puerto Rican prisoners, background on the independence struggle, calls for amnesty, and methods of support.

title: International Day of Support for the Puerto Rican POW Hunger Strike (May 26, 1981)
publisher: National Committee to Free Puerto Rican POWs
description: Bilingual mobilization document for the May 26, 1981 international day of support for the Puerto Rican POWs' hunger strike, including messages of solidarity with Irish liberation prisoners.

title: It's Time to Bring Them Home (1999) — Puerto Rican POWs Campaign
publisher: National Committee to Free Puerto Rican POWs and Political Prisoners
description: Bilingual organizing packet for the 1999 campaign to free the Puerto Rican political prisoners, including action items, sample letters, prisoner addresses, and biographies.

title: Capitalism Has Won a Game But Not the Championship — Benedetti / Buck (1990)
description: Essay by Uruguayan writer Mario Benedetti translated from Spanish by imprisoned anti-imperialist Marilyn Buck, reflecting on socialism after the Cold War.

title: Censored Women Speak — Marilyn Buck
description: Essay by Marilyn Buck on political censorship and women's imprisonment, arguing that imprisonment is itself a form of censorship; includes statements by Susan Rosenberg.

title: Medical Conditions and Treatment of Prisoners on O and X Wings, Soledad (1970)
description: Compilation of letters from prisoners describing medical neglect and abuse on the O and X wings of the Correctional Training Facility at Soledad.

title: Extra! Prisoners in Revolt! — Soledad Brothers Defense Committee
description: Soledad Brothers Defense Committee broadside covering the Folsom Prison strike demands, the Soledad 7 case, and prisoners' calls for racial unity inside California's prisons.

title: The Soledad Brothers Newsletter Vol. 1 No. 1 (1971)
description: Inaugural issue of the Soledad Brothers Defense Committee newsletter introducing the case of George Jackson, Fleeta Drumgo, and John Clutchette.

title: Trial of the Soledad Three — Village Voice (June 18, 1970)
description: Paul Cowan's June 18, 1970 Village Voice article examining the political context of the Soledad Brothers trial in Salinas, California.

title: Jalil Abdul Muntaqim Biography
description: Autobiographical statement from Black Panther Party and Black Liberation Army member Jalil Muntaqim (Anthony Bottom), one of the New York 3.

title: New York 3 Freedom Campaign Newsletter Vol. 1 No. 1 (1992)
description: First issue of the New York Three Freedom Campaign newsletter covering the cases of Herman Bell, Jalil Muntaqim (Anthony Bottom), and Albert Nuh Washington, former BPP/BLA members convicted in 1975.

title: Black Liberation Army Communique (November 5, 1981)
description: BLA Coordinating Committee communique issued shortly after the October 20, 1981 Rockland County Brink's expropriation, including an essay on the Strategic Alliance of the Armed Military Forces of the Revolutionary Nationalist and Anti-Imperialist Movement.

title: Break De Chains — National Committee for Defense of Chesimard & Squire (1973)
publisher: National Committee for the Defense of Jo Anne Chesimard and Clark Squire
description: Defense-committee pamphlet collecting messages and statements from imprisoned Black Liberation Army members, produced in support of Assata Shakur (Jo Anne Chesimard) and Clark Squire (later Sundiata Acoli).

title: Open Letter to the white Left in the U.S. — BLA
description: Open letter from BLA members Dhoruba Moore and Frank Khali Abney criticizing U.S. white leftists for pursuing reformist politics that perpetuate systemic oppression.

title: The Nature of Black Revolution — BLA Coordinating Committee
description: Theoretical statement from the BLA Coordinating Committee framing Black Revolution as a national-liberation struggle rather than a class-based proletarian struggle.

title: Assata Bulletin (May 2, 1977)
description: Defense-committee bulletin carrying a statement from Assata Shakur to her supporters during her 1977 New Jersey trial.

title: Assata-Sundiata Bulletin (August 1977)
description: Joint defense-committee bulletin covering Assata Shakur's New Jersey trial and Sundiata Acoli's confinement conditions.

title: Enemies of the State — Gilbert, Whitehorn, Buck (1999)
authors: David Gilbert; Laura Whitehorn; Marilyn Buck
description: Roundtable interview with imprisoned anti-imperialists David Gilbert, Laura Whitehorn, and Marilyn Buck reflecting on past movements, victories, errors, and conditions for contemporary revolutionary struggle.

title: From Attica to Abu Ghraib — Jericho Amnesty Movement (April/May 2005)
description: April/May 2005 Jericho Amnesty Movement booklet with reflections from U.S. political prisoners drawing connections between Attica, federal control units, and Abu Ghraib, with updates on incarcerated activists and prisoners of war.

title: Political Prisoners in the United States — Organizing Handbook (MXGM, 2001)
description: Malcolm X Grassroots Movement organizing handbook on U.S. political prisoners, including definitions, COINTELPRO background, biographies, and strategies for support work.

title: Working Paper on Federal Parole/Release for U.S. Political Prisoners and POWs (1989)
authors: Linda Evans; Susan Rosenberg; Timothy Blunk
description: Strategic working paper by imprisoned anti-imperialists Linda Evans, Susan Rosenberg, and Timothy Blunk analyzing federal parole and release mechanisms for political prisoners and prisoners of war.

title: Uses and Effects of Control Unit Prisons — Bonnie Kerness (1996)
description: Bonnie Kerness's compilation of interviews with incarcerated people, prepared for the National Campaign to Stop Control Unit Prisons' hearing on the use and effects of control units.

title: Allegations of Ill-Treatment in Marion Prison, Illinois (Amnesty International, May 1987)
description: May 1987 Amnesty International report documenting allegations of guard mistreatment of prisoners at USP Marion (the federal Control Unit), with correspondence chain regarding requests for an official investigation.

title: Pontiac Prisoners Support Coalition Newsletter No. 10 (December 1979)
description: December 1979 newsletter from the Pontiac Prisoners Support Coalition examining state tactics, trial proceedings, and calls for community participation in defense of the Pontiac Brothers.

title: Pontiac Prisoners Support Coalition Newsletter — Mass Death Penalty Trial
description: Pontiac Prisoners Support Coalition newsletter on the capital charges against the Pontiac Brothers, the historical context of the 1978 Pontiac rebellion, and concerns about jury bias.

title: Pontiac Prisoners Support Coalition Newsletter No. 13 (December 1980)
description: December 1980 newsletter reporting on jury selection, courtroom proceedings, prisoner correspondence, and case-dismissal victories for the Pontiac Brothers defendants.

title: Black Flag — Organ of the Anarchist Black Cross (Vol. 4 No. 13, 1977)
publisher: Black Flag Group / Anarchist Black Cross
description: Coverage of the murder of Spanish anarchist Laureano Cerrado Santos and an obituary of Aaron Rogat; also articles on the Sex Pistols, punks, trade unions, and press hysteria.

title: Black Flag — Organ of the Anarchist Black Cross (Vol. 4 No. 14, 1977)
publisher: Black Flag Group / Anarchist Black Cross
description: Reports on the Murray Trials and the release of Ronan Stenson, plus an article on the Hull prisoners' demonstration.

title: Black Flag — Organ of the Anarchist Black Cross (Vol. 4 No. 15, 1977)
publisher: Black Flag Group / Anarchist Black Cross
description: Focuses on the execution of Siegfried Buback and reviews Spain's transition from dictatorship to democracy.

title: Black Flag — Organ of the Anarchist Black Cross (Vol. 5 No. 1, 1977)
publisher: Black Flag Group / Anarchist Black Cross
description: Conflicts in Germany between anarchists and German police; debate over the role of trade unions across Europe.

title: Black Flag — Organ of the Anarchist Black Cross (Vol. 5 No. 3, February 1978)
publisher: Black Flag Group / Anarchist Black Cross
description: Contains a biography of Black anarchist political prisoner Lorenzo Komboa Ervin.

title: Black Flag — Organ of the Anarchist Black Cross (Vol. 5 No. 4, May 1978)
publisher: Black Flag Group / Anarchist Black Cross
description: Focuses on the post-Franco transition in Spain, featuring articles from the Spanish anarchist journal Bicicleta.

title: Black Flag — Organ of the Anarchist Black Cross (Vol. 5 No. 5, 1978)
publisher: Black Flag Group / Anarchist Black Cross
description: Retrospective on the 1968 reorganization of the Anarchist Black Cross.

title: Black Flag — Organ of the Anarchist Black Cross (Vol. 5 No. 6, October 1978)
publisher: Black Flag Group / Anarchist Black Cross
description: Focuses on conflicts with the police in West Germany and includes articles on feminist movements within the anarchist movement.

title: Black Flag — Organ of the Anarchist Black Cross (Vol. 5 No. 7, 1978)
publisher: Black Flag Group / Anarchist Black Cross
description: Vol. 5 No. 7 of the Anarchist Black Cross's Black Flag magazine.

title: Black Flag — Organ of the Anarchist Black Cross (Vol. 5 No. 8, May 1979)
publisher: Black Flag Group / Anarchist Black Cross
description: Vol. 5 No. 8 of the Anarchist Black Cross's Black Flag, May 1979.

title: Black Flag — Organ of the Anarchist Black Cross (Vol. 5 No. 9, July 1979)
publisher: Black Flag Group / Anarchist Black Cross
description: Contains Lorenzo Komboa Ervin's manifesto to the international anarchist movement and an article about the British punk band Crass.

title: Black Flag — Organ of the Anarchist Black Cross (Vol. 5 No. 10, 1979)
publisher: Black Flag Group / Anarchist Black Cross
description: Follows the British Persons Unknown trial (Taff Ladd, Vincent Stevenson, Ronan Bennett, Iris Mills, Trevor Dawton) and examines leftism in Great Britain.
